intro: Medusorchis andamanica, also known as Andaman orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid found in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Medusorchis andamanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
